A PHP and MySQL app concept for matching people around shared adventures and preferences.

## Overview

A full-stack app concept with users, profile photos, social links, adventure types, and preferences.

## What It Shows

- User accounts
- Profile customization
- Adventure creation
- Preference-based discovery

An arcade-style Unity game project focused on user-friendly interaction design, gameplay responsiveness, and neon-inspired visual feedback.

## Overview

Prairie Rush was a single-player game built in Unity for a User Interaction Engineering course. The project emphasized intuitive gameplay, responsive controls, and user-friendly UI design while maintaining a fun retro atmosphere.

## How to Access

Please contact me directly for the project's code. Otherwise, you can use the "Live Demo" link above, which will redirect you to my itch.io profile where you can download the game for Windows. It will say untrusted publisher at the moment, as I don't have formal credentials or enough people who have played the game yet.

## My Contributions

Although I was not the primary gameplay developer, I designed and programmed a significant portion of the project, such as: 
- Jumping
- Prairie-inspired textures
- Health bar and obstacle-hitting logic
- Integrating music
- Game Over Screen
- Accessibility Settings (such as turning "health bar flash" on/off and using less intense textures) 

I also integrated animations from an Asset store and refactored UI logic to make it more maintainable.

## User Interaction Engineering Goals

The primary goal of the course project was to design a user-friendly experience. This included:

- Clear visual feedback
- Easy-to-use controls
- Understandable menus and HUD elements
- Smooth gameplay interaction

## Technologies Used

- Unity
- C#
- Unity UI System
- Audio Integration
- Texture Design

## What I Learned

This project gave me hands-on experience with:

- Unity workflows
- Coordinating development within a team
- Designing interactions for usability
- Refactoring UI systems

A resilient message-driven workflow using MassTransit, RabbitMQ exchanges, queues, and consumers.

## Overview

This project demonstrates a message-driven backend where an API publishes work into RabbitMQ and consumers process it asynchronously.

## Highlights

- API producer pattern
- Exchange and queue routing
- Consumer retry strategy
- Architecture diagrams for documentation

Overview

This project demonstrates a message-driven backend where an API publishes work into RabbitMQ and consumers process it asynchronously.

Highlights

  • API producer pattern
  • Exchange and queue routing
  • Consumer retry strategy
  • Architecture diagrams for documentation
A rich-text writing app for creating, saving, and sharing documents with custom visibility controls.

## Overview
WordProcessor is a document-writing application that lets users create rich-text documents, save their work, and share documents with others based on visibility settings.

## Core Features

- Create and edit rich-text documents
- Save documents for later access
- Share documents with other users
- Set document visibility levels
- View documents based on access permissions

## Visibility Levels

### Public

Anyone with access to the site can view the document.

### Self-Only

Only the document owner can view and edit the document.

### Friends-Only

Only approved friends can view the document.

## Project Goal

The goal of this project is to create a clean, focused writing experience while also supporting controlled sharing. Instead of every document being either fully private or fully public, users can choose the level of visibility that fits the purpose of the document.
It was also designed to practice layered architecture for the first time.

## Example Use Cases

- A user writes a private journal entry and marks it as **Self-Only**
- A user creates a tutorial and marks it as **Public**
- A user shares a draft with friends by marking it as **Friends-Only**
- A user edits and saves a document over time
